programme.title: Rebelde
programme.desc: Six teenagers with different lives and personalities attend a prominent private school with only one thing in common: their vocation and passion for music.

programme.title: Capítulo 2
programme.desc: Roberta tries to thwart her admission. Miguel has trouble getting in to take the admission test. Thanks to her aunt, Lupita will attend school.

programme.title: Capítulo 3
programme.desc: Lupita's aunt helps Miguel get in to take the test and in exchange asks him to look after her niece. Miguel meets Mía and there's a mutual attraction.

programme.title: Capítulo 4
programme.desc: For Celina's sake, Mía pretends not to like Miguel. In Valle de Bravo, Roberta pretends to be drowning to get Mauro's attention. Diego's assaulted then saved by Miguel.

programme.title: Capítulo 5
programme.desc: Diego's attackers make it as if he was mugged. Miguel saves him and conceals the truth. Later Miguel struggles with Mía and pushes her over a cliff.

programme.title: Capítulo 6
programme.desc: On the edge of the cliff, Miguel humiliates Mía for what she did to Celina. Mía decides to hide what happened with Miguel and apologizes to Celina. Diego's father finds out about the mugging.

programme.title: Rebelde
programme.desc: Six teenagers with different lives and personalities attend a prominent private school with only one thing in common: their vocation and passion for music.

programme.title: Capítulo 8
programme.desc: Miguel asks Celina to be his girlfriend. Diego and Roberta are on a collision course. Everyone thinks Roberta's been writing the anonymous notes.

programme.title: Capítulo 9
programme.desc: Roberta pretends to be sick to get out of school. Lupita feels she doesn't fit in at school. Miguel knocks Nico unconscious for insulting him.

programme.title: Capítulo 10
programme.desc: Lupita wants a room change, but Roberta apologizes and tells her she wants to try to be friends. León threatens to send Diego to a military academy.

programme.title: Ofertín y los vinagres balsámicos
programme.desc: Albertano sale a la calle a buscar trabajo para que El Chino pueda ir a una excursión a los prismas basalticos, María se encuentra abatida ya que su abuelo quiere desaparecer la botarga de Ofertín quien baila junto a ella mientras anuncia sus ofertas, pues nadie se quiere meter dentro de esta, Albertano se ofrece gustoso y María le paga una semana por adelantado, con este dinero El Chino podría ir a su excursión. A la mañana siguiente, Albertano descubre que Doña Lucha ha tirado a la basura la botarga de Ofertín por error. Don Carmelo amenaza con demandarlos pero Rosa Aurora decide tomar el lugar del peluche y baila seductoramente lo que causa revuelo en la colonia e incrementa las ganancias de la tienda.

programme.title: La familia P. Luche
programme.desc: Follows a dysfunctional family in Peluche City.

programme.title: El súper episodio del súper
programme.desc: La familia Peluche va al supermercado y luchan por un lugar de estacionamiento. Federica se pone celosa de que Ludovico habla gustosamente con la señorita de la carnicería.

programme.title: El atraco
programme.desc: Ludovico roba a su amigo Flavio y es encerrado; Federica es llevada a la estación de policías a declarar; unos ladrones entran a la casa de la familia Peluche.

programme.title: Juego de roles
programme.desc: Federica intenta seducir a Ludovico pero él no se da cuenta y ella llora porque perdieron la llama de la pasión. Ludovico y Federica buscan como reavivar su matrimonio y hacen un juego de cambio de personalidad.

programme.title: Valle del nabo
programme.desc: El novio de Bibi, Manuel, la invita a Valle del Nabo a pasar el fin de semana. La familia de Bibi los alcanza en casa de Manuel. Manuel termina con Bibi por ser amargada y rara.

programme.title: Día de las madres
programme.desc: La familia P. Luche festeja el Día de las Madres de una manera muy particular. Ludovico intenta componer el baño pero un plomero muy guapo lo ayudará.

programme.title: Reparaciones
programme.desc: Federica contrata un carpintero guapísimo para arreglar una puerta. Ludovico se pone muy celoso de Sebastián, el carpintero, y quiere demostrar que él puede hacer las cosas mejor. Ludovico hace un desastre en su casa.

programme.title: El cumpleaños atrasado de Ludovico
programme.desc: Federica tiene un plan para hacer que Ludovico la lleve a comer a un nuevo restaurante. La familia Peluche conoce a Eduardo Santamarina. Todo sale muy mal en la cena.

programme.title: ¿Dónde está Rambo?
programme.desc: Pedro ha extraviado a Rambo y se deprime. La suegra de Alejandra quiere llevar a los López Pérez de vacaciones.

programme.title: Mollejitas 'Mi flaquita'
programme.desc: Arturo tiene la idea de poner un puesto callejero de comida. Pedro y Cris aplican técnicas de feng shui en el departamento para las buenas energías.

programme.title: Vecinos
programme.desc: In a neighborhood in Mexico City, the neighbors have problems and silly misadventures with one another.

programme.title: La ex de Rocko
programme.desc: Rocko tiene contacto con una exnovia y Alejandra se pone celosa. Los Rivers llevan a Luisito Jr. a algunos cástines.

programme.title: Tomasa
programme.desc: Luis compra un dispositivo inteligente para controlar el departamento y lo presume con los Vecinos. Los López Pérez rifan un extraño cuadro.

programme.title: La cruda
programme.desc: Rocko y Alejandra bebieron demasiado la noche anterior y no recuerdan nada de lo que pasó, Rocko fue arrestado por el robo de artículos para bebes y Luisito Jr. dio sus primeros pasos impresionando a sus padres de la nueva etapa que comienza.

programme.title: Prueba de embarazo
programme.desc: Alejandra descubre que está embarazada, ¿cómo lo tomará la familia? Chris y su pareja han decidido adoptar a un bebé.

programme.title: Melena de Luis
programme.desc: Luis debe recurrir a remedios extremos para poder continuar con su labor de venta. Rocco está dispuesto a fugarse de la prisión.

programme.title: Foto familiar
programme.desc: El abuelo de Benito llega al edificio para tomarse la foto familiar. Germán encuentra a un equipo de niños con quien hacer la reta de fútbol.

programme.title: Abarrotes Don Arturo
programme.desc: Morris tiene la tarea de vender unos chocolates de la escuela y a su familia se les ocurre una idea. Lorena manda a arreglar la máquina de escribir de Frankie.

programme.title: Tapa calefactora
programme.desc: Germán intentará instalar una nueva tapa calefactora en el inodoro de don Hipólito. Mientras que, Vanessa probará un remedio contra las canas.

programme.title: Regalo perfecto
programme.desc: Luisito le da un regalo a Silvita por su cumpleanos, pero ella no lo esperaba. En tanto que, Calaco decide visitar a Rocco en su casa.

programme.title: Tendedero
programme.desc: Magdalena decides to put a clothesline in the middle of the corridors of the building and Luis will look for a way to remove it.

programme.title: Amigos
programme.desc: Silvia agrega a Luis al chat de la guardería y Germán recibe la visita de sus amigos comentaristas de TUDN que transmiten un partido desde la conserjería.

programme.title: Auto nuevo
programme.desc: Luis le compra un auto nuevo a Silvia para que pasee con Luisito Jr. y Morris le tiene una mala noticia a sus padres: no le gusta el rock.

programme.title: Tacos veganos
programme.desc: Virginia y Borre terminan su relacion y no conforme, ella decide iniciar su propio negocio de tacos veganos. Arturo se encuentra un billete de mil pesos.

programme.title: Ropa de paca
programme.desc: Jorjáis decide invertir en un negocio de ropa de paca y descubre que no está solo, tiene competencia. Silvia y Luis buscan sobrevivir al primer día de guardería de Luisito Jr.

programme.title: Silvia party loving
programme.desc: Karla, la sobrina de Silvia, la visita en su casa, y ella finge que tiene una actitud muy cool y se va de parranda con ella. Pedro se siente mal y no puede salir de su cuarto.

programme.title: Nano por un día
programme.desc: La guardería está cerrada y Silvia y Luis no tienen quién cuide a Luisito Jr., por lo que tendrán que elegir entre los vecinos. Hipólito, Pedro y Germán buscan hacer válida la garantía de un celular.

programme.title: Sortilegio
programme.desc: Alejandro returns home after a terrible accident to discover that he is married to Maria Jose, a poor, innocent girl who was deceived by Alejandro's brother Bruno. Bruno used Alejandro's name to marry Maria Jose, soon after he sent to kill his brother in hopes that Maria Jose would inherit Alejandro's fortune. But when Maria Jose arrives at the mansion she realizes that Bruno lied to her and used her. She is also scared when Alejandro is found alive and returns home. Doctors tell Alejandro that he suffers from memory loss but he is convinced he never met Maria Jose before and goes in search of the truth, but during this time and Maria Jose actually fall in love, while Bruno and Maura (Alejandro's ex-girlfriend) plot to break them up. Meanwhile, the brothers' mother Victoria has fallen in love with Fernando--Alejandro's best friend, so closer to his age than hers.

Bruno used Alejandro's name to marry Maria Jose, soon after he sent to kill his brother in hopes that Maria Jose would inherit Alejandro's fortune. But when Maria Jose arrives at the mansion she realizes that Bruno lied to her and used her. She is also scared when Alejandro is found alive and returns home. Doctors tell Alejandro that he suffers from memory loss but he is convinced he never met Maria Jose before and goes in search of the truth, but during this time and Maria Jose actually fall in love, while Bruno and Maura (Alejandro's ex-girlfriend) plot to break them up. Meanwhile, the brothers' mother Victoria has fallen in love with Fernando--Alejandro's best friend, so closer to his age than hers.

programme.desc: Steven Rinella and special guest, the author Tim Ferriss, travel to the remote north slope of Alaska.

programme.title: MeatEater - The Greatest Draw: Alaska Dall Sheep Part 1
programme.desc: After drawing one of the most coveted big game tags in North America, a Tok Management Area Dall sheep tag, Steven Rinella heads out for a truly rare and epic Alaskan experience. Along with his brother Danny and friend Paul, Steve is dropped deep into the Alaska Range. After a rugged hike through nasty weather, Steve's skills and fortitude are tested against his elusive, high-climbing quarry.

programme.title: MeatEater - The Greatest Draw: Alaska Dall Sheep Part 2
programme.desc: After drawing one of the most coveted big game tags in North America a Tok Management Area Dall sheep tag Steven Rinella heads out for a truly rare and epic Alaskan experience. Along with his brother Danny and friend Paul, Steve is dropped deep into the Alaska Range. After a rugged hike through nasty weather, Steve's skills and fortitude are tested against his elusive, high-climbing quarry.

programme.title: MeatEater - Sonoran Super-Birds: Mexico Gould's Turkey
programme.desc: Steven Rinella crosses the border into Mexico's desert to call in the Gould's wild turkey. Of the five turkey subspecies in North America, the Gould's is the largest but rarest. On this unique Mexican adventure, Steve finds exciting action for gobbling toms. With locally made charcoal at his disposal, he'll grill up a big, brined, delicious bird.

programme.title: MeatEater - Straight Flush: Montana Mountain Grouse
programme.desc: Steven Rinella and his friend Ronny Boehme drive into the mountains of southwest Montana to hunt the high ridge tops for dusky grouse. Ronny's Bracco Italiano hunting dogs have been bred and trained for this work, but can Ronny handle the altitude? The duo prepares dusky grouse jalape√±o poppers and grouse fettuccine with a white wine and cream sauce.

programme.title: MeatEater - Meet the Meateaters: Montana Crew Muley Part 1
programme.desc: The goal on MeatEater has always been to make viewers feel like they're on a hunt with Steven Rinella, but on this two-part episode Rinella grants his viewers an all-access pass to see just how the MeatEater crew works together to make the show. Strap-in for some behind-the-scenes action.

programme.title: The Fantastic Pho
programme.desc: After Kevin quits his part-time gig as a supermarket security guard, Donna suggests that he try to make money by doing something he is passionate about.

programme.title: I'll Be Home For Christmas... Maybe
programme.desc: When he and Donna join Mott and his wife for a holiday dinner at Enzo's, Kevin's attempts to sympathize with over how hard it is to raise seven kids send Cindy running from the restaurant. Though Mott reassures them that this has happened before, when Cindy doesn't return the following day, he asks Kevin to babysit while he's at work. So with Kevin busy at Mott's, Kyle steps up to help Donna with her long list of Christmas chores. Struggling to keep Mott's kids under control, Kevin taps Chale to pretend he's one of Santa's elves and threaten to take Christmas away. With Cindy snowed in at a casino where she's hiding, Mott asks Kevin to help out for a few more days. Although Kevin apologizes for abandoning their family, when Donna assures him that everything's under control, he's hurt to find that Kyle has replaced him at home. After telling Mott that it's time to go back to his own family, Kevin is surprised by how quickly his kids stop paying attention to him once Cindy is back. But when he sees his family and Kyle celebrating without him, his attempt to win them back by sneaking onto the roof to put up a Christmas decoration ends with predictably disastrous results.

programme.title: Ring Worm
programme.desc: Kevin tries to make good on an overdue promise to buy Donna a proper engagement ring, but his plan backfires when he tries to surprise her.

programme.title: Kevin Vs. The Dutch Elm
programme.desc: After years of procrastinating, Donna insists that Kevin move their pool out from under the shade of their neighbor's elm tree. While Kevin is rounding up his friends to help, Duffy says it would be easier to cut the tree down rather than move the pool. Noting that the tree isn't on their property, Donna says she tried to get their neighbor, Mrs. Hoost, to cut it down without success. Confident that his conflict resolution skills will get the job done, Kevin goes next door to appeal to Mrs. Hoost, only to be turned away by her and her menacing son, Rutger. Though Kevin is resigned to moving the pool, upon learning that Mrs. Hoost is going to Florida, Donna suggests using an approaching storm as cover to cut the tree down. However, when they tell the kids about their plan, Kendra insists that they must first relocate a family of squirrels that is living in the branches. With Mrs. Hoost gone and the squirrels moved, Kevin and his crew prepare to pull the tree down with Duffy's truck. When Donna spots the initials that Mrs. Hoost and her late husband carved in the trunk years ago, she reconsiders the plan. But when Kevin tries to call it off, Duffy misunderstands and pulls the tree down into the Gables' pool.

programme.title: Choke Doubt
programme.desc: Kevin's life-saving act of heroism is overshadowed by Kyle's rescue of a cat from an apartment fire.

programme.title: The Back Out
programme.desc: Kevin and Donna jeopardize their chance for a kid-free vacation care of their chiropractor when he catches them using his colleague's services. Meanwhile, Kyle, still bitter that Kevin overlooked him to be best man at his wedding to Donna, tries to convince Chale to give him that responsibility.

programme.title: Kevin Can Wait - Pilot
programme.desc: A newly-retired police officer looking forward to spending more quality time with his family discovers he faces tougher challenges at home than he ever did on the streets.

programme.title: Kevin And Donna's Book Club
programme.desc: Having deemed his friends boring, Kevin wrangles an invitation to accompany Donna to her hard-partying book club.

programme.title: Sleep Disorder
programme.desc: To ease the pain of his aching shoulder, Kevin tries reversing a 20-year-old habit of sleeping on his left side until he turns over and discovers that Donna is not a pretty sleeper. But after he convinces her to switch sides, he realizes that she's set up the bedroom to her advantage. When further investigation reveals that the rest of the house is also arranged for her benefit, Kevin's friends point out that she's removed everything from his comfortable recliner to his George Foreman Grill. Meanwhile, Chale's emotional reaction to a cooking show has Kendra thinking he needs more male friends. After listing all the problems with the house, Kevin says he's going to make some changes, beginning with a beanbag chair in the bathroom. Though Donna says that it's taken years to get everything just right, she agrees that, from now on, any changes will be mutual. However, after Kevin tells his pals that everything is fine now that Donna let him move his coffee mug, they counter that he's given in too easily. Meanwhile, to toughen Chale up, Kendra arranges for him and Kyle to watch cage fighting on TV. But when they hear the tragic family story of a fighter's life, both of them break down crying. Taking command of the situation, Kevin moves his old recliner into the kitchen and reclaims his grill from the attic. After Donna finds him in the bedroom with his meatloaf-scented candle and compression pants, she warns Kevin that it´s all going back into storage. Though Kevin refuses to back down, he's forced to call Donna for help when he gets stuck in the compression pants. As Kevin concedes that Donna has done a great job with the house, Chale's unusually stoic reaction to a heart-wrenching movie worries Kendra. But as Chale reveals that he knows she was trying to toughen him up, he breaks down and cries after she admits that she doesn´t want him to change.

programme.title: Kevin's Bringing Supper Back
programme.desc: Kevin insists that the family bond over Sunday dinner, unaware that his friends are planning a big surprise that same night.

programme.title: Chore Weasel
programme.desc: Because everyone else is busy, Donna tells Kevin he needs to skip his football game with the guys so he can be at home when their new armoire is delivered. Though Kevin tells them there's no way out of it, when his teammates note that they planned this game against a team of rookie cops six months ago, Kyle suggests hiring a "Chore Weasel" to sign for the delivery so he can play. Returning home nursing an injured ankle, Kevin is surprised to find that their Chore Weasel Blake not only signed for the armoire but assembled it, too. So when Donna arrives to see what he says he accomplished and gives him a list of other chores, Kevin calls Blake. When Chale comes home unexpectedly to find Blake on the roof cleaning the gutters, Kevin insists that he tell Donna that he did it. After Donna shows up, too, Kevin orders Blake to sneak out of the house so he can claim credit for his work. Delighted that Blake will do whatever he wants, Kevin invites him to an Islanders hockey game after his friends bow out because of their football injuries. However, when Donna suspects something's up, she and Kendra force Chale to reveal the truth about the Chore Weasel. After a fun night at the game, Blake is dropping Kevin off at home when Donna suddenly appears outside the car window. Caught in his lie, Kevin confesses to taking credit for Blake's effort and promises to tell Donna if she asks him to do something he doesn't want to do. However, though he thought he invited Blake to the hockey game as a friend, Kevin is surprised when he gets a bill for his time.

programme.title: Kevin's Good Story
programme.desc: As Kevin and Donna are out for dinner with Goody and his wife Didi, a discussion of their upcoming anniversary turns to the time Kevin miraculously survived being pushed off a roof by a robber he was chasing. But there's just one problem: although Kevin told Donna that he walked away from the fall, the truth is that Goody fell and then asked Kevin not to say anything, fearing that Didi would make him quit the force. Worse still, it isn't the only heroic story Kevin has stolen from his partner. As Kevin looks to make amends for embarrassing Goody in front of his wife, his alleged heroism inspires Didi to ask him to speak to her fourth grade class. Unable to talk his way out of it, Kevin confesses to his parish priest and asks how to stop perpetuating the lie. Told to come clean, Kevin tells Didi the truth, but asks his friends not to say anything to Donna. However, after confronting Goody about lying to her for years, Didi insists that Kevin tell his wife, too. After softening her up on their anniversary with dinner at a Moroccan restaurant and a show featuring the music of Gloria Estefan, Kevin tells Donna that he lied about the fall. Pressed to explain, he admits that not only was it Goody who fell, but that he had to be rescued when he got stuck on a fence during the chase. Finally, after forgiving Kevin, Donna makes a confession of her own when she admits that she didn't say anything when he forgot their anniversary years ago because she forgot it, too.

programme.title: Beat The Parents
programme.desc: Finding a new friend for Jack lands Kevin and Donna in an uncomfortable relationship with the boy's unbearable parents.

programme.title: Who's Better Than Us
programme.desc: Kevin is hurt by how easily his old partner has replaced him with a rookie cop who can do no wrong.

programme.title: Hallow-We-Ain't-Home
programme.desc: When Kendra, Chale, Sara and Jack don costumes and head to the high school for a parking lot event called Trick-or-Trunk, Kevin convinces Donna to abandon tradition and not give out Halloween candy this year. Turning off all the lights to make it look like no one's home, they hunker down with popcorn and wine to watch a movie. Though they resist the temptation to answer when trick-or-treaters come knocking at their door, they don't fool neighbors Joey and Paul Gruber. Meanwhile, as Jack complains that no one understands his clever costume, Chale and Kendra's romantic Halloween is threatened by the loud music coming from a neighboring car at Trick-or-Trunk. After watching the Gruber kids cover their car in shaving cream, vandalize their birdbath and steal the welcome sign her grandmother made, Donna joins Kevin to get revenge. However, when they sneak into the backyard to engage in some vandalism of their own, Kevin and Donna discover they've walked into the Grubers' carefully laid trap. Meanwhile, when he goes over to demand that their noisy neighbors turn down their music, Chale is chased away by a large Viking's angry girlfriend. While taking refuge in the Grubers' playhouse, Donna wonders how they could merit such harsh treatment. Forced to come clean, Kevin confesses to upsetting Rick after ruining his leaf blower and then calling his wife Judy fat. Though annoyed to learn about the feud, Donna gives him permission to break the welcome sign so they can escape from the playhouse. But when they walk into another ambush, Kevin's apology to the Grubers falls on deaf ears.

programme.title: The Power Of Positive Drinking
programme.desc: Although Donna wants his undivided attention when complaining about her tough day at work, Kevin is focused on choosing a combination grill-smoker for the backyard. After saying no to the new barbeque, Donna asks him to talk to Kendra about missing too much school in order to take on more shifts at Enzo's. However, as things get busy and Kevin pitches in to help, Enzo hires him to be his new bartender. When the positive feedback he gets for his advice to customers leads Kevin to spend more and more time at Enzo's, it comes at the expense of his family and friends. So after he starts getting calls at the house, Donna buys him the smoker in order to get him to stay home. Though Kevin insists that he can keep up with his responsibilities, after he forgets to pick up Donna at the dentist, she insists that he quit the job. After fantasizing about turning his bartending advice into a motivational speaking career, Kevin shows up at Enzo's to get his last check, only to be sidetracked by a brother and sister seeking his input about reconnecting with their estranged father. Once he finally arrives at home, he finds himself facing an intervention. Confronted with his failures at advising his family, friends and Enzo's customers, Kevin is forced to reconsider his career choice.

programme.title: Pod Save America (David Sedaris is Mostly Bark, Some Bite)
programme.desc: Legendary humorist David Sedaris stops by the studio to talk to Lovett about his new book of essays, "The Land and Its People," his father's support for Trump, and what he learned about liberals when he was bitten by a dog. They also unpack Sedaris's frustration with being labeled "queer," his nonnegotiable rule about what not to discuss in a relationship, and his unique approach to growing older.

programme.title: Lovett or Leave It (Xi’s Just Not That Into You)
programme.desc: China blows smoke up Trump’s skirt, Rand Paul’s son gets hot under the collar, and Marco Rubio slips into something a little more comfortable. This week, Outlander star Sam Heughan and Lovett explore online fandom and only one survives. Hacks’ Caitlin Reilly sweeps us off our feet and into the distant past, and we all journey to cloud nine for a hilarious round of Second Thoughts.

programme.title: This F*cking Guy (Bari Weiss: Everything You Didn't Know About Her Sh*tty Past)
programme.desc: In our 40th episode of This F*cking Guy, Erin dives deep into the origins of anti-woke crusader turned CBS News Editor-in-Chief Bari Weiss. From her origins as an antagonizing student at Columbia University to a career of ill researched op-eds and launching her own media company, to running CBS News and her relentless campaign to promote Zionism, this may be our most manipulative guy yet!

programme.title: Offline with Jon Favreau (Has The Left Finally Figured Out the Internet?)
programme.desc: Ever heard a popular TikToker wax poetic about who they’re voting for? They might be getting paid…and not disclosing by whom. Kyle Tharp, author of the Chaotic Era newsletter, joins Offline to discuss the emergence of pay-for-play political influencers and whether Democrats have made any progress in figuring out the internet. He and Jon talk about the decline of Ben Shapiro and the Daily Wire, the new success of legacy media on TikTok, and the intense grassroots anger around data centers.

programme.title: This F*cking Guy (Laura Loomer: Everything You Didn't Know About Her Sh*tty Past)
programme.desc: In our 34th episode of This F*cking Guy, Erin and Alyssa dive deep into the past of the unhinged MAGA clown, Laura Loomer. From filming undercover for Project Veritas, to cozying up to eco-fascists, to melting down over her Twitter ban, to being Trump’s number 1 fan girl, this crazy b*tch may be our nuttiest guy yet!

programme.title: Strict Scrutiny (Ballrooms, ‘Bama and (Very) Bad Behavior)
programme.desc: Leah, Kate, and Melissa recap another busy week in legal news, covering the continued fallout from the Voting Rights Act case, Louisiana v. Callais, the ongoing saga of the DOJ’s insurrectionist slush fund, wild twists with the Broadview Six, more ballroom drama, the curious case of the Georgia judge who had loud sex in her chambers and then lied about it, and more. They also cover SCOTUS opinions involving compassionate release for prisoners and compelled arbitration before Leah speaks with University of Michigan law professor Barbara McQuade about her book, The Fix: Saving America from the Corruption of a Mob-Style Government.

programme.title: Pod Save America (The Splintering MAGA Media Rage Machine)
programme.desc: Will Sommers, author of The Bulwark's "False Flag" newsletter, sits down with Tommy to talk about the MAGA media world — including the right-wing personalities who may be considering a political future (Tucker Carlson and Candace Owens), those whose influence is crumbling (Ben Shapiro), and some of the most troubling — and strangest — rising voices. Tommy and Will then break down the ways some of MAGA's leading voices are abandoning the president, what their world will look like and whether it can even survive in a post-Trump future.

programme.title: The City Kids
programme.desc: Oliver and Lisa play host to four children from the city, who find themselves in Hooterville as part of a rural exposure program. The kids learn how to milk the cow, pick vegetables, plant seeds, and share sleeping quarters with four-legged friends.

programme.title: The Coming Out Party
programme.desc: Little Lori Baker, who after coming to stay with the Douglases on a rural experience program, is staying on at the Douglas farm. This city girl, like Oliver, has found that she loves the country life! Lisa decides to throw Lori a welcome party.

programme.title: A Royal Love Story
programme.desc: Lisa tells a rather fanciful version of how she met and fell in love with Oliver. The Douglases' house guest, little Lori Baker from the city, is all ears. The tale begins with how Lisa's father, the king, was exiled, causing Lisa to flee to Paris.

programme.title: Oliver Goes Broke
programme.desc: The residents of Hooterville assume that Oliver Douglas must be broke when Lisa is spotted looking for work and standing in line at the soup kitchen. But assumptions in this town, even logical ones, are bound to be wrong.

programme.title: The Great Mayoralty Campaign
programme.desc: There is the old adage that warns never to discuss politics with your spouse... but what if you are both running for the same office?! That predicament comes true when Oliver and Lisa face each other in the Hooterville mayoral election.

programme.title: Eb's Double Trouble
programme.desc: Lisa and Oliver try to sort out of the troubles of fools in love. The problems begin when Eb invites both his girlfriend and another young woman to the same dance, while Arnold the pig falls for not one, but two attractive bovines.

programme.title: Apple Picking Time
programme.desc: Oliver is having a tough time. He has no one to help him pick the apples from his orchard, and he's promised to teach Lisa how to drive. The neighbors are all injured from assisting other harvests, and Lisa has a run-in with a sheriff.

programme.title: Enterprising Eb
programme.desc: Eb feels the time is right to settle down and marry Darlene. However, he has nowhere near the money it will take to make a down payment on a house. With the help of the Douglases, who have given him two acres of land, Eb applies for a loan.

programme.title: The High Cost Of Loving
programme.desc: Eb believes he has found his destiny when an accounting course he has enrolled in turns out to be acting workshop. All the world's a stage. Ed dives into acting and turns every simple interaction at the Douglas farm into a starring role.

programme.title: The Liberation Movement
programme.desc: After Lisa attends a Women's Liberation League meeting, she gives Oliver a scolding for denying her equal rights and demands to swap chores. While Lisa does a stellar job at Oliver's work, Oliver's attempts at hers leave something to be desired.

programme.title: Charlie, Homer And Natasha
programme.desc: Oliver refuses to take Lisa to New York City to attend the wedding anniversary of an old friend. Meanwhile, Eb, always up to something, has a new invisible friend named Charlie. To get her way, Lisa introduces her own invisible friend... Natasha.

programme.title: The Engagement Ring
programme.desc: Lisa gives the financially-challenged Eb her engagement ring to present to Darlene, his longtime sweetheart, so that he can pop the question. The ring is reportedly valued at $2,000, however, when Darlene's father has it appraised, it's a shocker.

programme.title: The Free Paint Job
programme.desc: The Douglases are offered a free paint job on their house and barn so long as they allow the paint company free advertising on their barn wall. Meanwhile, Lisa tries to make spaghetti for Oliver's upcoming birthday. Will it prove im-pasta-ble?

programme.title: Son Of Drobny
programme.desc: Lisa hosts the son of WWII hero Drobny, and all of Hooterville gets excited in anticipation of a surge of tourists. Trouble is, this hero was a duck, as is his son. Oliver tried to warn everyone they were making too much fuss over feathers.

programme.title: The Wedding Deal
programme.desc: Young lovers Eb and Darlene are just weeks away from their wedding day, but they must choose the marriage venue. It's hard to decide between a church and a car wash. At least it is for Eb, who is still looking for a way to pay for it all.

programme.title: Star Witness
programme.desc: While staying with the Douglases, Arnold the Pig witnesses a bank robbery when he goes with the Douglases on an errand in Pixley. When Lisa asks him to describe the two robbers, Arnold, reliving the moment, loses his voice from fear.

programme.title: A Girl For Drobny
programme.desc: Lisa worries about Drobny the duck when the water fowl stops eating and sleeping and suffers headaches. Eb suggests the duck needs a duck girlfriend. Lisa agrees and goes about town trying to the right partner for Drobny.

programme.desc: Undercover police officers fight crime in L.A. schools.

programme.title: Divine Elegance
programme.desc: Bob paints a mountain scene in a faux matte.

programme.title: Cliffside
programme.desc: Sea waters thunder and mist against cliffs.

programme.title: Autumn Fantasy
programme.desc: Bob paints a peaceful autumn scene.

programme.title: Old Oak Tree
programme.desc: Bob paints a massive oak.

programme.title: Winter Paradise
programme.desc: Bob paints a tranquil winter scene.

programme.title: Lurch's Grand Romance
programme.desc: Lurch suffers from unrequited love over Tiny Trivia (guest star Diane Jergens), Morticia's visiting school chum who only has eyes for show business. The entire family decides to try to turn their stolid butler in to a new personality.

programme.title: Ophelia's Career
programme.desc: When Ophelia laments that she has been jilted, Morticia suggests a career as an alternative to marriage. She tries chemistry first (with explosive results) then turns to operatic singing. Cousin Itt is summoned to coach her to stardom.

programme.title: The Addams Family Goes To School
programme.desc: Morticia and Gomez decide to keep Wednesday and Pugsley home from school after Morticia sees the fairy tales they have to read. When the truant officer comes to investigate, he is shocked at what he finds.

programme.title: Morticia And The Psychiatrist
programme.desc: Morticia calls a psychiatrist after she fears Pugsley is becoming normal after he abandons his pet octopus for a puppy and is seen wearing a Boy Scout uniform, playing with a baseball bat, and helping an old lady cross the street.

programme.title: Fester's Punctured Romance
programme.desc: Uncle Fester, who has become lonely, convinces Gomez and Morticia that it is time for him to find a bride. He decides to put an ad in the newspaper, looking for love. But things go awry when a door-to-door saleswoman comes by.

programme.title: Gomez, The Politician
programme.desc: Election fever grips the Addams household, as Gomez (John Astin) supports the council candidate whose campaign promise is to drain the town's disgusting bogs. Morticia (Carolyn Jones), who likes "the lovely bogs," labels the candidate an extremist.

programme.title: The Addams Family Tree
programme.desc: Pugsley and Wednesday start a row at a party when their gift of a tarantula upsets Harold, the birthday boy. Harold's parents insist that the Addams children lack breeding and, therefore, are no longer allowed to play with their son.

programme.title: Morticia Joins The Ladies League
programme.desc: After Pugsley befriends Gorgo, a circus gorilla, Gorgo decides to follow Pugsley and Gomez home, where he is accepted as one of the family. But Gorgo gives Morticia's tea guests, the Ladies League members, quite a shock.

programme.title: Halloween With The Addams Family
programme.desc: A pair of bank robbers are welcomed as Halloween trick-or-treaters by Morticia and Gomez. The creepy atmosphere of the house, Morticia's smoldering holiday punch and Lurch's ominous presence impel the crooks to abandon their plans.

programme.title: Green Eyed Gomez
programme.desc: When Lionel Barker, a former suitor of Morticia, shows up, Gomez is jealous. But he's soon calmed when he learns that Barker is after Mildred, the new maid. The Addamses then discover that Barker is a con man--after Mildred's money, not her love.

programme.title: New Neighbors Meet The Addams Family
programme.desc: Newlyweds move in next door. But the honeymoon loses its tranquility when Uncle Fester enters their house through a tunnel and they flee in terror at the sight of Lurch, Grandmama sharpening her ax, and Morticia and Gomez bathing the octopus.

programme.title: Wednesday Leaves Home
programme.desc: The family faces a crisis when Wednesday packs her spider and runs away (after being scolded for playing with Uncle Fester's TNT). When they try to file a missing person report, a detective is anxious to retire early due to their story.

programme.title: The Addams Family Meets The V.I.P.'s
programme.desc: Official visitors from an unfriendly nation receive the shock of their lives when they decide to exploit the Addams Family as an average uncultured family. The visitors are shaken up by Morticia's carnivorous plants among other things.

programme.title: Morticia, The Matchmaker
programme.desc: Morticia's cousin, Melancholia, wants to marry Clarence P. Harvey after most of her marital prospects have joined the Foreign Legion. Clarence recoils when Melancholia puts on a campaign to win him, backed by Morticia, Gomez and Clarence's boss.

programme.title: Lurch Learns To Dance
programme.desc: Lurch becomes a veritable gazelle when he takes dancing lessons at Gomez and Morticia's suggestion, after he admits to being a wallflower at social functions. Each member of the Addams Family teaches Lurch a different step.

programme.title: Art And The Addams Family
programme.desc: Grandmama's paintings leave an art critic speechless, so Gomez sends for his friend, Sam Picasso, to teach Grandmama to paint in the abstract. The starving artist ignores Grandmama's and Morticia's work as he eats and paints.

programme.title: The Addams Family Meets A Beatnik
programme.desc: Rockland Cartright III is a beatnik who crashes his motorbike into a tree and recuperates at the Addams house. Each regards the other as an oddity, although the family learns to appreciate certain aspects of Rockland's life.

programme.title: 41 Harsh Truths Nobody Wants To Admit
programme.desc: Alex’s Twitter has been one of my favourite sources of insights over the last few years. Today we get to go through some of his best lessons about life, human behaviour, psychology, business and resilience again. And as always this is so, so good. Expect to learn the skills needed to thrive in todays every changing world, why a few bad days shouldn’t ruin the rest of your year, how Alex’s mind has changed about work and happiness in the past year, how to figure out what you want in life, the rare dynamic between Alex and his wife Leila, why having a work life balance isn’t all it’s cracked up to be, why more money won’t buy you more happiness, how to get a top tier girls and much more…

programme.title: Sharp Truths From A British Comedian
programme.desc: Having made his career out of cutting jokes and brutal roasts, today Jimmy Carr reveals a more introspective side. Jimmy opens up about his favorite parts of life, the best bits of his show, and the wisdom he's picked up along the way. Expect to learn why Jimmy is such a fan of the show, and how his thinking has been influenced but the MW podcast ecosystem, how to know what you should be doing with your life, ways to overcome anxiety and dealing with the balance of meaning and pleasure, what life is like on the road for Jimmy, how Jimmy has learned to enjoy his time better, and where he finds gratitude, how fatherhood changed Jimmy for the better, how to become someone worth becoming, and much more…

programme.title: Living with Confidence & Going All In
programme.desc: Expect to learn why Matthew says life often “rhymes”, why it’s okay if you do everything right and still not get the result you want, how Matthew views the role courage plays in his life, how to learn to deal with failure, the difference between a nice guy and a good man, the most important principle Matthew refuses to compromise on, the appropriate balance of running toward a life you want vs. away from one that you fear, how to balance self-confidence with humility, the difference between “being” and “acting”, what it means to be more impressed with the wow instead of the how, rumors on Matthew’s return to the True Detective franchise, and much more…

programme.title: How To Find Direction When Nothing Feels Right
programme.desc: From 6× Mr. Olympia to full-time business owner and stay-at-home dad, it’s safe to say Chris Bumstead’s been enjoying retired life. So what’s he been up to this past year, what’s changed most for him, and what’s he looking forward to next? Expect to learn how retirement is going for Cbum, if Cbum would have retired last year even if he didn’t win, how Chris learned to sit and process his emotions, if Cbum regrets retirement and if he misses the competing yet, how Chris plans on leveling up for baby Bumstead number two, he hardest part of losing that “Mr. Olympia structure” in daily life, if Chris ever plans on returning the stage again, and much more…
